title: Indonesia Has to Put A Sum of Money in BRICS’ New Development Bank to Join
body: Indonesia recently announced its intention to join the BRICS-backed New Development Bank (NDB).

title: Trump Slaps 25 Pct Tariff on Imported Cars, Expects $100B Revenue
body: Trump imposes a 25% tariff on auto imports, aiming to boost US manufacturing. Critics warn of price hikes, trade tensions, and job losses.

title: Eid Travel and Spending Decline in 2025, Government Pushes Stimulus
body: Consumer spending and travel for Eid 2025 decline, prompting government stimulus efforts to sustain economic growth and purchasing power.

title: QJ Motor to Invest $10M in West Java Assembly Plant to Expand Indonesian Market Share
body: As part of its expansion strategy, QJ Motor aims to grow its dealership network from the current eight outlets to 20 across the country.

title: Jakarta Exempts Houses Under Rp 2 Billion from Property Tax
body: Pramono also clarified that no similar tax relief will be offered for vehicle ownership.

title: France, EU Partner to Make Indonesia's Busiest Ports Safer
body: France and EU's Global Ports Safety specifically targets Jakarta's Tanjung Priok and Surabaya's Tanjung Perak.

title: Putrama Wahju Setyawan Appointed BNI CEO
body: He succeeds Royke Tumilaar, who led significant reforms to enhance BNI’s digital services and expand its global presence.

title: France's Macron to Visit Indonesia in May for Critical Mineral, Food Investment
body: French top diplomat Jean-Noël Barrot says that his president Emmanuel Macron will also bring some new investment in transportation.

title: BYD’s 2024 Revenue Hits $107B, Surpasses Tesla’s Sales
body: BYD’s 2024 revenue hit $107B, surpassing Tesla’s $97.7B. Strong EV sales and a new fast-charging system fuel its global expansion.

title: Rupiah Hits Lowest Level Since 1998 Crisis Amid US Tariff Fears, Fiscal Concerns
body: Indonesia’s rupiah weakens to its lowest level since 1998, pressured by global trade risks and US tariff concerns. Investors remain wary.

title: JCI Jumps 1.21% as Market Welcomes Danantara's New Executives
body: Jakarta stocks surged 1.21% as investors welcomed Danantara’s new leadership.

title: Australian Health Tech BrainEye Sets Sights on Indonesian Sports Industry, PSSI Deal
body: BrainEye lets footballers screen for concussions using an AI-driven eye-tracking technology.

title: Indonesia Remains Hopeful of $20 Billion JETP Climate Funding Despite US Exit
body: Despite the optimistic outlook, Jakarta is aware that the JETP financing process needs to pick up pace.

title: New BRI President Hery Gunardi Well-Known for Leading Major Bank Mergers
body: Hery takes over from Sunarso, under whose leadership BRI expanded its national footprint, including launching its own satellite system.

title: Government to Merge SOEs into Agriculture and Fisheries Holding Agrinas
body: Several ideas have emerged, including the development of 20,000 hectares of coastal fishponds and 1 million hectares of palm plantations.

title: Hery Gunardi Takes Helm at BRI
body: State lender Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BRI) appointed Hery Gunardi as its new president director, replacing Sunarso.

title: Jakarta Stocks Extend Losses as Investors Seek Safety in Bonds
body: Jakarta’s stock index tumbled 1.55 percent, marking a second straight loss as investors shifted to bonds amid global trade uncertainty.

title: Tourism Sector Feels the Heat as Government Slashes Spending
body: Government budget cuts are hitting Indonesia’s hotel and tourism industries hard, forcing businesses to slash costs and seek incentives.

title: Full List of Danantara’s Organizational Structure: Thaksin Shinawatra, Ray Dalio Included
body: Other prominent names include Columbia University economist Jeffrey Sachs and top equity portfolio manager F. Chapman Taylor.

title: BRI and BCA Control 29% of Nationwide Public Savings
body: Third-party funds in banks reached Rp 8,612.5 trillion ($520.1 billion) as of February 28, marking a 5.1 percent increase year-on-year.

title: Jakarta to Operate Chinese-Made Electric Commuter Trains
body: Each carriage is required to complete 4,000 kilometers of trial runs before being cleared for regular operation.

title: Fore Coffee Targets $23M IPO as Indonesia’s Coffee Market Booms
body: Fore Coffee plans a $24M IPO to fund expansion, adding 140 stores in two years as Indonesia’s premium coffee market sees rapid growth.

title: JCI Slumps Nearly 2 Pct as Fed Signals Caution, China Uncertainty Lingers
body: JCI fell 1.94% to 6,258.1 as US job data, FTSE rebalancing, and China policy uncertainty drove investors to risk-off mode.

title: New Polish Ambassador Calls Indonesia 'Important Economic Partner'
body: Government data shows that Indonesia-Poland trade had jumped from $997.1 million in 2023 to $1 billion the following year.

title: Gov't Told to Be Careful with Foreign Business Deals after Navayo Dispute
body: Senior minister Yusril says that the government should pay attention to the dispute settlement chapter in a foreign business deal.

title: Thai Ambassador Leads Business Delegation to Nusantara to Explore Investment Opportunities
body: The Indonesian government envisions Nusantara as a sustainable and environmentally friendly city.

title: Prabowo Wants Batang Industropolis to Be Indonesia's Shenzhen
body: Batang Industropolis became the first special economic zone (SEZ) for Prabowo to launch since assuming power last October.

title: President Prabowo Moves to Calm Markets After JCI’s 6 Pct Plunge
body: President Prabowo seeks to reassure investors after the JCI plunges 6.12%, arranging a meeting to restore market confidence.

title: Prabowo to Inaugurate Batang Special Economic Zone
body: The president will also review the progress of the Two Countries Twin Parks project, a collaboration with China’s Fujian Province.

title: Japan Seeks to Hire More Indonesian Workers in Fishery Sector
body: Japan has been facing a significant decline in its fishery workforce, dropping from 238,000 workers in 2003 to 123,000 in 2022.

title: Indonesia’s Richest Woman Loses $3.6 Billion in Three Days
body: Earlier this month, Marina had a net worth of $7.5 billion after the company’s stock surged beyond the upper trading limit.

title: Government Earmarks Rp 20 Trillion to Rescue Labor-Intensive Industries
body: The textile industry alone employs over four million people and generates more than $2 billion in exports annually.

title: Travel Spending During Eid Holidays Estimated at Rp 375 Trillion
body: Widiyanti cited a recent survey by her ministry, which estimates that the holiday period will see 146 million trips.

title: Indonesia, Russia to Have Trade Talks Ahead of Prabowo-Putin Meeting
body: Chief Economic Affairs Minister Airlangga Hartarto and Russian First Deputy Prime Minister Denis Manturov will co-chair this meeting.

title: Bank Indonesia Keeps Interest Rate Steady at 5.75 Pct as Expected
body: The central bank also decided to maintain a 5-percent deposit facility rate.

title: Indonesia, EU Still Can't Agree on Trade Pact's Import Permit
body: Indonesia and the EU have been negotiating a comprehensive economic partnership agreement (CEPA) since July 2016.

title: Frugal Ramadan Spending Puts Pressure on Indonesia's Economy
body: Indonesia’s Ramadan economy slows as weaker consumer spending, mass layoffs, and deflation curb growth amid economic uncertainty.

title: Bayan Resources Shuts Down Australian Unit in Corporate Streamlining
body: Bayan Resources (BYAN) dissolves its Australian unit Kangaroo Minerals in a corporate restructuring move, assuring no impact on operations.

title: OJK Eases Buyback Rules to Stabilize Market
body: Indonesia’s OJK allows listed firms to buy back shares without shareholder approval, aiming to stabilize stocks amid market volatility.
